title: Tokyo 3 Structures
wikitext:
[]: Tokyo-3 Structures             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sun Jan 06, 2008 8:20 pm We all know that the buildings in Tokyo-3 actually can rise and lower into the GeoFront when the city's under attack, or some other reason. But the point is, that, what actually holds the structures in place; wouldn't it put too much pressure onto the restraints to hold them down in the GeoFront? And, how heavy are the buildings, exactly Furthermore, do the structures actually rise or lower to accommodate the Evangelion's size, which was pointed out by Sachiel_13? For example, in Episode 2, Eva 01 is seen as similar size and dimensions to the armament structures, but in Episode 12 while chasing the Angel Sahaquiel, the buildings are like miniatures compared to the Evangelions. Is it just for dramatic effect, or is it just so NERV set the buildings down a little to not hinder the Evangelion's running style/speed? Some images to help you discuss about it. Tokyo-3 GeoFront-Structures Lowered http://sandalettes.free.fr/anime/Evangelion/geofront.jpg Tokyo-3 Above Ground-Eva 01 Running http://content.answers.com/main/content/wp/en/thumb/6/6c/150px-EVA01_in_Tokyo.jpg Eva 01 in Episode 2-Tokyo-3 http://www.nervarchives.com/images/icon.200x150.faceoff.jpg Took me ages to find these pictures, but they'll help you. Also, one with Eva 00, battle with Leliel http://www.animeinfo.org/featured/eva/pics/eva00-1.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 06, 2008 8:22 pm I didn't say they raised and lowered to accommadate, but ok. :)

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 06, 2008 9:35 pm You're hot-linking, Bee-Zerk. Host the images on a free image-hosting account if you're going to do that. Not furnished with the awesome ability to take screencaps off DVDs, eh?

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 06, 2008 9:38 pm Those pictures are extremely small. Think you can find bigger?

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sun Jan 06, 2008 10:06 pm First time I've ever done this. @Reichu: I have no idea how to do that. And I did the best I could...and since it's the first time I've ever done this, you might as well do it for me for your own good. :P

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Jan 06, 2008 10:18 pm BEsERk EVA01 wrote: @Reichu: I have no idea how to do that.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sun Jan 06, 2008 10:23 pm Wow, you said it yourself, Reichu. And I really can't be bothered to take images off my DVD to take screen captures, let alone putting them on PhotoBucket. Youth never comes again in life. I must cherish it and not waste it. Does it really matter? You could've just sent me a PM, and not post it on this thread! This is about Tokyo's Armament Structures! Red bold text is for the admins. Fifty lashes with a wet noodle for you.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 06, 2008 10:32 pm I'm not even going to say anything to that.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Sun Jan 06, 2008 10:42 pm Just get back on topic will you? I'm already trying my best to not go off topic and spam. And Sachiel_13, that comment didn't do anything to help contribute the thread. This thread is about Tokyo-3! For god's sake! You're ruining it for me!

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Sun Jan 06, 2008 10:52 pm BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Just get back on topic will you? I'm already trying my best to not go off topic and spam. And Sachiel_13, that comment didn't do anything to help contribute the thread. This thread is about Tokyo-3! For god's sake! You're ruining it for me!

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Jan 07, 2008 6:24 am I think that the structure are able to move up and down using somekind of toothed wheel attached which rotate either way to move the blocks up and down, (pretty durable) and those circular lock mechanisms to keep it in place. If not, they are probably on somekind of a railing with locks, and probably moves up using some thing acting as a wheel.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Mon Jan 07, 2008 6:26 am I wonder if there's been any malfunctions of the system, excepting any Angel blast attacks to it. Wow, if those pulleys can sustain an Eva-sized gun and umbilical cable, I'm not surprised if they've been malfunctions.

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Mon Jan 07, 2008 6:30 am BEsERk EVA01 wrote: Wow, if those pulleys can sustain an Eva-sized gun and umbilical cable, I'm not surprised if they've been malfunctions.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Mon Jan 07, 2008 6:47 am But they crumble in attack.

[]:             Quote Post by Operation Yashima » Tue Jan 08, 2008 10:18 am The Imperialist wrote: But they crumble in cinematics .

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Tue Jan 08, 2008 1:09 pm We get a clearer view of how the buildings are lowered in 1.0. Some of the buildings have truncated sides that are smooth and metallic, maybe they are lowered using electromagnets - there are such things as magnetic elevators. Also, some of the buildings have the numbers 1 to 22 written on the side, which implies the floors line up with the 22 levels of armour plating protecting the GeoFront. In Stingray they used hydraulics to lower the city as all the fortifications and weapons were raised to the surface.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Tue Jan 08, 2008 1:53 pm I think there are mechanical wheels somewhere, and those railings could be to make sure that it doesn't go in or out the wrong way. If it was hydraulics, then if the building were destroyed, there would have been sprays of water coming out, but that never happened.

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Jan 08, 2008 7:21 pm The Imperialist wrote: I think there are mechanical wheels somewhere, and those railings could be to make sure that it doesn't go in or out the wrong way. If it was hydraulics, then if the building were destroyed, there would have been sprays of water coming out, but that never happened.

[]:             Quote Post by BEsERk EVA01 » Wed Jan 09, 2008 2:18 am I never knew the buildings could actually move that fast with hydraulics and all...and then stop them almost instantly and lock them. Sweet. The future rules.

[]:             Quote Post by The Imperialist » Wed Jan 09, 2008 3:28 am If they can do that, they must have a hell of a lot of water, and a very rigid locking system.
